user_query:
I’m standardizing short captions for my hostel’s video reels. Please help me build a small, testable workflow that validates and then improves the sample messages.

Inputs (provided in the workspace):
- input/style_guide.md
- input/hostel_features.html
- input/messages.jsonl

What I need:
1) Write a deterministic Python CLI validator at tests/validate_messages.py that reads:
   - The rules from the fenced YAML block named "rules" inside input/style_guide.md.
   - The feature names from <li class="feature"> elements within input/hostel_features.html.
   - A messages JSONL file with objects of shape {"id": str, "text": str}.

   The validator must enforce these checks per message using the parsed rules:
   - OVER_CHAR_LIMIT: text length exceeds char_limit.
   - BANNED_WORD: text contains any banned_words (case-insensitive if case_insensitive=true).
   - CTA_MISSING: no allowed_ctas phrase is present (case-insensitive) when cta_required=true.
   - CTA_NOT_ALLOWED: a CTA-like phrase is present but not exactly one of allowed_ctas (case-insensitive) when cta_required=true.
   - FEATURE_MISSING: no feature name (from the HTML) is mentioned in the text (case-insensitive) when require_feature_mention=true.

   The CLI should accept --messages, --style, --features, and --report arguments and output a JSON report at the given --report path with structure:
   {
     "summary": {"total": int, "passed": int, "failed": int},
     "results": [
       {"id": str, "pass": bool, "violations": [str], "char_count": int}
     ]
   }

   Also write the extracted features (as a sorted list of strings) to output/features_extracted.json so I can see exactly what was parsed from the HTML.

2) Run the validator on input/messages.jsonl and save the baseline report to output/baseline_report.json.

3) Rewrite the messages for tone, clarity, and brevity to comply with the style guide. Keep the same ids, only change the text. Requirements for each rewritten message:
   - Must be at or under the char_limit from the style guide.
   - Must include exactly one of the allowed_ctas (verbatim, case-insensitive match accepted).
   - Must mention at least one feature name extracted from input/hostel_features.html.
   Save the rewritten set to output/rewritten_messages.jsonl (same JSONL schema as input).

4) Re-run the validator on output/rewritten_messages.jsonl and save the final report to output/final_report.json, which should show all messages passing (failed=0).

Deliverables to produce under the workspace:
- tests/validate_messages.py (the CLI validator)
- output/features_extracted.json (list of features parsed from the HTML)
- output/baseline_report.json (validation results on the original messages)
- output/rewritten_messages.jsonl (your improved messages)
- output/final_report.json (validation results on the rewritten messages; expect all pass)
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
